rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LALANNE VS. MICHELE ARMANINO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Robert Lalanne'S Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 7, 2013, Line 15, Petitioner Robert Lalanne's Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award The arbitration award is confirmed. Pursuant to CCP 1286, the Court will confirm the award "as made." The Court denies Petitioner's request to enter two judgments that alter the award; nor will the Court address other issues concerning the enforcement or interpretation of the award. The Court will sign a form of judgment simply confirming the award and with the award attached as its Exhibit A. The Court declines to stay execution of the judgment pursuant to CCP 918(a). The Court notes with displeasure that Petitioner filed a 26-page reply brief without permission in flagrant disregard of California Rules of Court 3.1113(d), (e), and (f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD S RESMA et al VS. UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYDS OF LONDON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 7, 2013, Line 5, DEFENDANT MCLARENS YOUNG INTERNATIONAL INC, CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S OF LONDON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants' motion for summary judgment is granted. The insurance policy provided for a two-year statute of limitations. (UF #13.) Plaintiffs' causes of actions accrued on May 20, 2008, when the last check omitting their names was issued. Plaintiffs filed the complaint on December 27, 2011, more than two years after their causes of actions accrued. Plaintiffs' claims against McLarens Young fail because that defendant owed no contractual or common law duty to Plaintiffs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: YAN KALIKA et al VS. MICHAEL SCHIMMEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton Andmoiton To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Eric Roe At Deposition Adn To Enforce The Court'S Pripr Orders Compelling Eric Roe'S Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions And Contempt Orders
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 7, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiffs YAN KALIKA'S and AA CONDOS LLC'S Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony Of Eric Roe At Deposition And To Enforce The Court's Prior Orders Compelling Eric Roe's Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions And Contempt Orders Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Defendant Eric Roe is ordered to attend his deposition and produce documents at Plaintiff's counsel's office no later than October 21, 2013. Defendant Eric Roe shall within 5 days pay to Plaintiff all sanctions that were previously awarded by the Court's orders of December 24, 2012, February 6, 2013, and July 17, 2013 and that remain unpaid. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff's supporting declaration does not provide sufficient facts for the court to determine an appropriate amount of monetary sanctions. Plaintiff's request for contempt sanctions and for Mr. Roe to be jailed is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO N.A. VS. GLENDA CASHMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 7, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT GLENDA CASHMAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant's demurrer is overruled; 10 days to answer. In the absence of a probate proceeding or trust notice procedures, the rights of a creditor of the decedent against the assets of the decedent's revocable trust are "as otherwise provided by law" (Cal. Prob. Code §19008) on liability arising from contract (§19000(c) and (a)(1)). That includes the right to proceed against trust assets to the extent the probate estate is inadequate. (§19001(a)) The Complaint alleges the necessary facts to bring the claim within §§ 19001 and 19008. Even assuming the 1 year statute of limitations under CCP 336.2 applies, Plaintiff's action was brought within one year. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HARPER VS. THE EPSTEIN GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 7, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT THE EPSTEIN GROUP'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration The motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of the arbitration. Paragraph 17 of the parties' fee agreement requires binding arbitration. Plaintiff's opposition papers do not show why the arbitration clause (1) is unenforceable or (2) does not apply to the instant action. The Court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance to January 2014. While plaintiff provides a doctor's note explaining that he has difficulty traveling, plaintiff fails to explain why he cannot appear by telephone. A petition to compel arbitration should be adjudicated in a summary manner under CCP 1290.2. The Court continued the matter from October 1, 2013, to give plaintiff time to make arrangements to appear by telephone. Plaintiff states that he has a mental disorder that makes it difficult to concentrate. Plaintiff has not provided competent medical evidence to support that assertion. The Court notes that plaintiff filed this action while representing himself on July 30, 2013. That filing, plaintiff's oppositions, and requests for default indicate that plaintiff can engage in the litigation process. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
